title: WISER Act of 2025
plain_summary: The WISER Act of 2025 helps women veterans who were forced to leave military service between 1951 and 1976 because they became pregnant, had a baby, or became a parent through adoption or custody. These women were kicked out of the military under an old rule called Executive Order 10240. The bill creates two programs to help them: one upgrades their military discharge records to make them better, and another gives them a one-time payment of twenty-five thousand dollars. Women veterans must apply for these programs, and if a veteran has died, her surviving spouse can still apply for the money.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Disability Assistance and Memorial Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Women Involuntarily Separated Earning Remittance Act of two thousand twenty five or the WISER Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Programs to Provide Compensation Benefit and Discharge Status Upgrades for Certain Veterans Who Are Women Discharged Pursuant to Executive Order 10240
sections.text: The Secretary of Veterans Affairs and the Secretary of Defense must create two programs to help women veterans who were discharged under Executive Order ten thousand two hundred forty. The first program upgrades their discharge status. The Secretary of Veterans Affairs will work with the Secretary of Defense to run this program if Congress approves money for it. Women veterans who want to participate must fill out an application that includes the information and promises the Secretaries require. When a woman veteran gets her discharge status upgraded through this program, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs must treat her as if she completed all her military duties when she was separated from military service. The second program provides a one time payment of twenty five thousand dollars. The Secretary of Defense will run this program. If a qualifying woman veteran dies after this law starts, her surviving spouse can apply for the money instead. People who want to participate must fill out an application with the information the Secretary of Defense requires. Congress can approve whatever money is needed to pay for this program. There are two types of eligibility rules. First, there is an absolute guarantee of eligibility for women veterans who were involuntarily separated from military service because of Executive Order ten thousand two hundred forty. This means their eligibility cannot be challenged. Second, there is a likely eligibility that can be challenged for women veterans who had a baby, got custody of a child, or adopted a child within ten months after leaving military service, or who had an incomplete pregnancy including from an abortion or miscarriage within those same ten months. In this section, active military naval air or space service means what that term means in section one hundred one of title ten of the United States Code. A covered veteran means a woman veteran who served in the military between April twenty seventh nineteen fifty one and February twenty third nineteen seventy six.
